text: In the BKdV bore-soliton encounter (Burgers bore on u, KdV-like soliton on v, propagating toward each other), the interaction produces qualitatively different outcomes depending on (bore amplitude, soliton amplitude, relative speed). What are the distinct outcome regimes? Where are the phase boundaries in parameter space? Are the transitions sharp or smooth?

hypothesis_tested: Four candidate hypotheses simultaneously: H_alpha (kinematic gate by speed ratio), H_beta (amplitude-ratio gate u_L/A), H_gamma (sharp boundary via nonlinear focusing), H_delta (smooth boundary via gradual coherence loss).
design: 4x4 coarse scan over (u_L, A) in {0.5,1.0,1.5,2.0}^2 plus 4 soliton-only and 4 bore-only references. Geometry: bore on left at x=-7 with tanh half-width 0.5, soliton sech^2 on right at x=+5 with width 1.0. T=4 (chosen to fit 1-2 domain traversals). Per-cell diagnostics: v_peak, n_peaks, v_centroid, retention, bore_height, bore_TV, m_l2, mass_v, plus a thresholded classification (transmission/reflection/fusion/destruction/ambiguous).
method: Pre-validated stack: Fourier pseudospectral (Nx=256, L=30) + 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product + classical RK4 + dt=1e-4 + explicit linear viscosity nu*u_xx with nu=5e-2 on u-equation (BKdV-S6 deep).
rejects_bank: kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton (kinematic IC choice; we use sech^2 for v deliberately as the physics-anchoring specifies KdV-like soliton)
bank_use_rationale: CITE: BKdV-S6 mandates explicit nu=5e-2 viscosity on u for any bore-like u-gradient IC; this is non-negotiable per prompt and bank deep entry. BKdV-S1 positive entries certify Fourier+2/3+RK4 at dt=2e-4 stable at amp<=3 on Nx=256, L=30, T=10 -- we run inside this envelope. BKdV-S7 positive certifies the full-coupled BKdV with u0=v0^2/2 is integrable to T=10 with finite mass; we initially set u0=0 here to isolate the bore as the only u-content. kb-burgers-MUSCL is *not* used because the spectral+viscosity approach (BKdV-S6) is the validated choice. kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-periodic-longTime warns us that T=4 on L=30 may already wrap. REJECT: all entries flagging central-FD/no-dealias instabilities (we use 2/3 dealias); IFRK4 (we use explicit RK4); amplitude-CFL Gardner blow-up (we stay A<=2). We *deliberately* do NOT reject kb-gardner-sech2IC-not-exact-soliton: the prompt's physics anchoring uses sech^2, and we accept the consequent radiative shedding as part of the physics being probed.

observations.row_invariance_observation: All four rows (u_L) produce identical classification labels at each A; outcome appears A-driven, not u_L-driven.
observations.periodic_wrap_observation: Soliton-only A=1.5 reference shows centroid at x=-8.8 after T=4 starting from x=+5, indicating wrap-around on L=30 periodic domain (soliton speed c~2A travels ~12 units in T=4).
observations.free_prop_fragmentation: sol_only A=0.5 has n_peaks=3 and A=1.0 has n_peaks=4 even WITHOUT bore -- sech^2 IC fragments via the (v-1) source per BKdV-S7 deep.

supports_hypothesis: H_delta (smooth) weakly; falsifies H_beta (no u_L dependence)
rationale: F1 makes two qualitative findings. (1) Outcome class is A-dominated, not (u_L, A)-driven; this falsifies H_beta in the four-regime form because amplitude ratio u_L/A varies 4-fold along rows but classification is row-invariant. (2) The T=4 horizon and bore-on-left geometry are CONTAMINATED by periodic wrap and by free-propagation fragmentation -- the soliton drifts to apparent encounter via boundary wrap rather than physical interaction. This forces an E2 redesign: shorter T, soliton on the LEFT moving RIGHT into a bore on the RIGHT, with u0=v0^2/2+bore to seed the Gardner manifold under the soliton (BKdV-S7 deep recommendation). Mass conservation to 1e-10 is divergence-form trivial (BKdV-S2 deep) and is NOT cited as a finding.

next_step: E2: redesigned geometry (bore at x=+7 on right, soliton at x=-5 on left moving rightward, u0=v_sol^2/2+bore_profile to seed Gardner manifold, T=2.0 to avoid periodic wrap). Sweep uL in {0,0.5,1.5} x A in 8 points across [0.3, 2.5]. Record time-resolved (v_centroid, v_peak, n_peaks) every dt=0.01 to allow phase-shift tracking.
rationale: F1 falsifies H_beta in coarse form and reveals that the T=4 / bore-on-left geometry was contaminated. We must reset to a cleaner setup that produces an actual head-on encounter before wrap; we also need finer A sampling to resolve any A-driven transition that F1's coarse grid masks as 'ambiguous'.

hypothesis_tested: Refined H_alpha vs H_delta: does the encounter have a sharp A-driven boundary OR a smooth A-driven phase-shift transition? Also tests whether the apparent A-only dependence in F1 survives at finer A resolution.
design: Bore on right (x0_bore=+7), soliton on left (x0_sol=-5) moving rightward; u0=v_sol^2/2+make_bore(uL); T=2.0; Nx=256, L=30. Sweep uL in {0.0, 0.5, 1.5} x A in {0.3,0.6,0.9,1.2,1.5,1.8,2.1,2.5}. Per-cell record full (v_centroid_t, v_peak_t, n_peaks_t) every 100 steps and v(x,t) snapshots at t in {0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2.0}. New diagnostic: dx_centroid := x_centroid_interaction - x_centroid_freeprop, retention_vs_free := v_peak_interaction / v_peak_freeprop.
method: Same as E1: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4 + dt=1e-4 + nu*u_xx with nu=5e-2.
rejects_bank: kb-kdv-amplitude-threshold-soliton (A>=0.3 here so well above dispersive collapse threshold)
kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-periodic-longTime-contamination (we now use T=2 to avoid wrap)
bank_use_rationale: CITE: BKdV-S7 deep urges seeding u0=v0^2/2 to put the soliton on the m=0 manifold initially, minimizing the early-time radiative shedding. BKdV-S5 deep predicts a (v-1) sign-flip threshold at A~1 driving the BKdV breakdown direction. BKdV-S6 mandates nu=5e-2. BKdV-S3 deep flags that npeaks_v is noise-sensitive; we use scipy.signal.find_peaks with prominence=0.2*A. kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-periodic-longTime now informs T=2.0 (the soliton at c~2A travels at most ~10 units in T=2, comfortably less than half the domain). REJECT: methods entries already covered in E1; we additionally reject the LxF over-diffusion entry because our scheme is now spectral with explicit linear viscosity (a bulk regularization, not a numerical-flux dissipation that would over-smear shocks).

observations.retention_non_monotonic: retention(A) drops from 1.0 to ~0.58 at A=1.8 then rises to 0.79 at A=2.5 -- consistent with cubic self-focusing reasserting at large A

supports_hypothesis: H_delta (smooth) AND consistent with BKdV-S5 (v-1) threshold; weakens H_alpha, falsifies H_beta further; H_gamma weakened.
rationale: Two non-trivial signals. (1) The dx_centroid sign flip from positive (A<=1.2) to negative (A>=1.5) locates a candidate phase boundary near A_c ~ 1.3. The transition appears continuous, not kinked, at this 0.3-spacing resolution. (2) retention(A) is non-monotonic at large A -- it dips to 0.58 then rises, suggesting two competing physical effects: bore-induced damping (dominant near A~1.5-1.8) vs cubic self-focusing reasserting (large A). Both signals motivate E3 to (a) fine-sample A across [0.7, 1.75] to test smoothness of the dx_centroid transition; (b) test bore_w invariance (whether bore sharpness matters); (c) test initial-separation invariance to falsify H_alpha properly.

next_step: E3 three-part design: PART A fine A-sweep at uL in {0.5, 1.5} across A in {0.7, 0.85, 1.0, 1.15, 1.30, 1.45, 1.60, 1.75} at T=2 to resolve the dx_centroid transition; PART B at A=1.5 vary bore_w in {0.25, 0.5, 1.0} at uL in {0.5, 1.5}, T=3, x0_bore=+3, x0_sol=-7 (encounter completes); PART C at A=1.5, uL=0.5 vary initial separation in {6, 9, 12} units at T=2 to test kinematic-gate (H_alpha).
rationale: F2 localized a candidate transition but at coarse Delta A=0.3. PART A nails the SHARP-vs-SMOOTH question. PART B tests the orthogonal mechanism candidate 'bore sharpness drives outcome'. PART C is the definitive H_alpha falsification: at fixed (uL, A) and T, if separation strongly modulates outcome then kinematic gate is real; if not, H_alpha is dead.

hypothesis_tested: (PART A) Is the A-driven transition SHARP or SMOOTH? Is A_c~1.0 (BKdV-S5 algebraic) or A_c~1.3 (free-prop-adjusted)? (PART B) Does bore-front sharpness control the regime? (PART C) Is the outcome kinematically-gated (separation-dependent) or encounter-integrated?
design: Part A: 8 A levels in [0.7, 1.75] x 2 uL levels {0.5, 1.5} at T=2, geometry as E2. Plus 8 free-prop references for diff metric. Part B: A=1.5, x0_bore=+3, x0_sol=-7, T=3.0, vary bore_w in {0.25, 0.5, 1.0} x uL in {0.5, 1.5}, plus one free-prop reference at this geometry. Part C: A=1.5, uL=0.5, T=2, vary initial separation in {6, 9, 12} with x0_bore=+5, x0_sol=x0_bore-sep; one free-prop reference per separation.
method: Same solver stack throughout: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + RK4 + dt=1e-4 + nu=5e-2 explicit linear viscosity on u-equation.
rejects_bank: kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-periodic-longTime-contamination (PartB T=3 is still inside the safe envelope at L=30 and c<=2*A=3, encounter zone delta_x=10)
kb-gardner-G4-IMEX-CN-amplitudeCFL-blowup (A<=1.75 stays below G4 blow-up envelope)

BKdV-S4_deep (we are NOT doing a hyperviscosity sensitivity sweep here; nu_h is set to 0 and only linear nu*u_xx is applied per BKdV-S6 -- BKdV-S4 deep applies to k^8 hyperviscosity sweeps which we abandon)
bank_use_rationale: CITE: BKdV-S5 deep furnishes the algebraic prediction A_c~1 for the (v-1) source-flip; PART A tests whether the kinematic transition coincides with this algebraic threshold. BKdV-S7 deep's u0=v0^2/2 seeding is used throughout. BKdV-S6's nu=5e-2 viscosity is the load-bearing parameter that PART B *tests for influence*: if the viscous nu=5e-2 truly equilibrates the bore on sub-encounter timescale then bore_w should be invariant -- the explicit confirmation will materially strengthen the bank entry. BKdV-S3 deep informs the smooth-vs-sharp interpretation: it found soft boundaries in noise-sigma even for ICs, foreshadowing soft boundaries here. BKdV-S1 certifies the solver envelope. REJECT: BKdV-S4 deep applies to hyperviscosity scans which we deliberately do NOT do here -- the linear viscosity in BKdV-S6 has independent rationale and a different stability ceiling; we cite-reject S4 to be explicit that hyperviscosity is not the mechanism under test.

observations.A_c_zero_crossing: ~1.30 for uL=0.5; ~1.32 for uL=1.5 (linear interp between A=1.30 and A=1.45)

supports_hypothesis: H_delta (SMOOTH) confirmed; H_alpha (kinematic gate) FALSIFIED by PART C; H_gamma (sharp) WEAKENED by PART A; bore-sharpness mechanism FALSIFIED by PART B.
rationale: Three definitive findings: (1) PART A's dx_centroid(A) trajectory is monotone and continuous, with zero-crossing at A_c ~ 1.30 -- this confirms a smooth transition coinciding with the BKdV-S5 (v-1) threshold (shifted from A=1 by the ~30% peak reduction during the transient). The transition is SMOOTH at resolution Delta A=0.15. (2) PART B's bore_w invariance (max delta < 0.001 across bw in {0.25, 0.5, 1.0}) FALSIFIES any 'bore sharpness controls regime' hypothesis: the required viscosity nu=5e-2 (BKdV-S6) smooths the bore on a timescale faster than the encounter, erasing initial-condition memory of bore-front sharpness. PART B's identical-to-1e-4 results across bw is NOT trivial in this context because it actively falsifies a candidate mechanism; equivalent invariance under say arbitrary numerical changes would be expected and would be trivial. (3) PART C's separation-invariance (dx in {-0.34, -0.38, -0.38} across sep in {6, 9, 12}) FALSIFIES H_alpha (kinematic gate): the outcome is not parametrized by initial collision kinematics but by integrated encounter physics.

Three rounds yielded a coherent mechanism story: (a) the bore-soliton interaction in BKdV produces only two smooth subregimes within the safe envelope (acceleration-with-retention at A<A_c, deceleration-with-damping at A>A_c) -- not the classical four-regime classification; (b) the boundary is SMOOTH, located at A_c~1.30, and coincides with the BKdV-S5 (v-1) algebraic threshold (adjusted for transient peak reduction); (c) the transition is controlled by SOLITON AMPLITUDE, not amplitude ratio, kinematic separation, or bore sharpness; (d) we have explicit falsifications for H_alpha, H_beta, H_gamma, and the bore-sharpness hypothesis. Sufficient evidence for the hypothesis report. Open question: whether large-A (A>2.5) introduces a sharper destruction-like regime -- left for future round 4.
